review:
The current regime is unambiguously Bear with a Deep dislocation reading, and today's composite signal has flipped to a full-corroboration Buy — a meaningful shift from the persistent No-Action stance that has dominated the past several weeks. The week ending June 7th was severe: BTC shed 16%, ETH 20%, and SOL 22%, compressing Fear and Greed to 12, a level associated historically with capitulatory exhaustion rather than orderly distribution. The dislocation signal is not noise; it reflects derivatives fear running materially ahead of any incremental fundamental deterioration, which is precisely the setup the Tepper framework is designed to identify.

Zooming out to the one-month and one-quarter windows, the picture is one of entrenched bearish persistence with only sporadic Buy signals — four in the past month, five year-to-date against sixty-one No-Action readings. Average BTC composite scores in the low forties indicate the market has spent the bulk of the quarter in compressed, oversold territory without generating the reflexive bid that typically follows. The funding rate z-score holding near neutral throughout suggests this is a spot-led decline rather than a leveraged unwind, which historically implies a slower base-formation process but also reduces the risk of a disorderly secondary leg down driven by derivatives cascades.

The YTD cross-asset rotation picture is instructive. ETH/BTC z at minus 1.37 and SOL/BTC z at minus 1.62 represent persistent, deepening underperformance in risk-proximate assets relative to BTC — consistent with institutional capital consolidating into the highest-liquidity layer of crypto rather than rotating into thematic or application-layer bets. This is mid-bear-cycle behavior in a post-halving year where the typical re-acceleration window has not yet materialized.

What makes today's signal genuinely interesting is the cross-asset context: equities are reading Business As Usual, with credit calm and VIX at a moderate 66th percentile. The crypto stress is therefore idiosyncratic, not a read-through from broad risk-off. That divergence is the dislocation. A stabilization in spot BTC above current levels with funding remaining neutral would confirm the setup; a credit spread widening or SPY continuation break would instead suggest the signal is premature.
